prompt: Play this short four-step World 2 capstone chain.

steps.prompt: Step 1: Hero is on the button and villain is in the big blind. Who is in position?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. The button acts later and keeps position on the big blind.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. On the button against the big blind, hero acts last after the flop, so hero keeps the in-position advantage for later streets.
steps.why_v1: Position means acting later and seeing the other player act first.

steps.prompt: Step 2: Keep the same seats. Hero opened preflop and villain called. Who has initiative on the flop?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. Hero raised last preflop, so hero keeps initiative into the flop.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. Hero raised preflop, so hero is still the last aggressor and keeps initiative until villain changes the line with aggression.
steps.why_v1: Initiative usually belongs to the player who made the last aggressive action.

steps.prompt: Step 3: Keep the same scene. The flop comes Jh Th 9c. Which action matches the more pressure-building texture?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. A connected two-tone flop supports the more pressure-building action.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. Jh Th 9c is dynamic and draw-heavy, so the pressure-building line fits better than treating it like a calm flop.
steps.why_v1: Connected two-tone boards create more draw pressure and more reason to keep the pressure on.

steps.prompt: Step 4: Keep the same flop. Hero holds Qh 8h and still has initiative. Which simple action fits best now?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. Initiative plus a strong draw on a dynamic flop supports the more assertive follow-up.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. Qh8h on Jh Th 9c gives hero a strong draw plus initiative on a dynamic board, so raising pressures weaker hands and keeps fold equity alive.
steps.recap_v1: Read the whole scene in order: position, initiative, board pressure, then the action that fits the pressure and draw strength.
steps.why_v1: When position, initiative, and a strong draw line up on a dynamic flop, the more assertive action fits the whole scene.
